top_categories.workout_context: 160 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
14,764ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 14,764ft of climbing on paved Ardennes roads with punchy, steep côtes does to your power file on race day.
160 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge's paved Ardennes roads with punchy, steep côtes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 160 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 160 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
92ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Ardennes roads with punchy, steep côtes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
At Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
Tempo is Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 160 miles on paved Ardennes roads with punchy, steep côtes. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 160 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 160 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 160 miles of paved ardennes roads with punchy, steep côtes in Banneux, Belgium.
